concerns.text: The 'ESO UVES Quasar Spectra' entry lists '*NO VERIFIED SOURCE*' and admits no verified URL exists. While the plan proposes a simulated fallback, the primary scientific claim (FR-001, US-1) requires ingestion of real ESO UVES spectra. A plan that cannot cite a real, accessible source for its primary data modality (quasar absorption spectra) is methodologically infeasible for the stated research question. The 'Verified Datasets' block must be updated with a canonical ESO Science Archive URL (e.g., https://archive.eso.org/scienceportal) or a specific Large Programme ID to prove the dataset is real and accessible.

The 'SDSS DR Galaxy Density' entry lists '*NO VERIFIED SOURCE*'. While marked as auxiliary, the spec (US-3) requires correlating Δα/α with large-scale structure. Without a verified source URL (e.g., SDSS SkyServer or DR16 release page), the feasibility of this specific analysis step cannot be validated. Replace '*NO VERIFIED SOURCE*' with the specific SDSS data release URL.

The 'NIST Atomic Spectra Database' entry lists '*NO VERIFIED SOURCE*'. While the plan notes hardcoded values, the Constitution (Principle II) requires all external citations to be verified. Replace '*NO VERIFIED SOURCE*' with the canonical NIST URL (https://physics.nist.gov/PhysRefData/ASD/) to satisfy the Verified Accuracy gate.
